content:
You are now a domain expert in requirements analysis.
You need to complete the following workflow:

Step 1: Read and understand the requirement document, and generate structured extraction results.
Step 2: Based on the extracted requirements, generate the actions that exist in the system.
Step 3: Based on the extracted requirements, generate the scenarios that exist in the system. Each scenario should be composed of actions.

First, complete Step 1: read and understand the requirement document and generate structured extraction results.
Below is a requirement document. Read and understand this document, complete the following tasks, and output the results according to the provided [Output Format].

#1 Identify the model description in the requirement document and provide a natural language introduction of the model

[Output Format]
Model Name:
Model Description:

#2 Analyze the document content and extract all requirement items

[Output Format]
Requirement Item 1:
    Requirement Description:
    Original Text:

Requirement Item 2:
    Requirement Description:
    Original Text:
...

#3 Extract the model input/output variable information(only in the table, do not construct new params), filling in the original text

[Output Format]
Variable 1:
    Variable Name:
    Type: integer / real / boolean
    Description:
    Port Type: Input / Output
    Is Constant: Yes / No

Variable 2:
    Variable Name:
    Type: integer / real / boolean
    Description:
    Port Type: Input / Output
    Is Constant: Yes / No
...

#4 Analyze the document content and extract the model’s Test Config information, directly filling in the original text

[Output Format]
Test Configuration 1:
Test Configuration 2:

Key Note:
Do not add any characters that are irrelevant to the required output format in the output.

1) Finite State Machine
Model:  ‘fsm_12B.mdl’
Description:  this model represents an abstraction of detailed design requirements for an Advanced Autopilot System interacting with an independent sensor platform.  The Finite State Machine (FSM) represents a cyber-physical system with two independent components executing in real time for the purpose of ensuring a safe automatic operation in the vicinity of hazardous obstacles.  The autopilot system, tightly integrated with the vehicle flight control computer, is responsible for commanding a safety maneuver in the event of a hazard.  The sensor is the reporting agent to the autopilot with observability on imminent danger.  When the conditions are met to allow an autopilot operation (supported and no failures) and the pilot is not manually controlling the vehicle (standby is false), the autopilot will behave normally until a maneuver is required in the event of a hazard (sensor is not good).
===== TABLE 1 =====
Input Scope	Name	#	Type	Description
Global	standby	1	Boolean	True when the pilot is in control of the vehicle
Global	apfail	2	Boolean	Failure indication from an external source
Global	supported	3	Boolean	Indication of general health of system, must be True to enable operation
Global	limits	4	Boolean	External indication to sensor on faulty condition impeding safe operation.
				
Output Scope	Name	#	Type	Description
Global	pullup	1	Boolean	True when the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state
Requirements:
Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).
The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is not good.
The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).
The autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.
The autopilot shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control (not standby).
The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to MANEUVER when a failure occurs (apfail).
The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to FAULT when limits are exceeded.
The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request).
The sensor shall change states from FAULT to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request) and limits are not exceeded (not limits).
The sensor shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the autopilot is requesting support (request) and the autopilot reports the correct active mode (mode).
Test Config:
1. t = 10

Model Name: fsm_12B.mdl
Model Description: This model represents an abstraction of detailed design requirements for an Advanced Autopilot System interacting with an independent sensor platform. The Finite State Machine (FSM) represents a cyber-physical system with two independent components executing in real time for the purpose of ensuring a safe automatic operation in the vicinity of hazardous obstacles. The autopilot system, tightly integrated with the vehicle flight control computer, is responsible for commanding a safety maneuver in the event of a hazard. The sensor is the reporting agent to the autopilot with observability on imminent danger. When the conditions are met to allow an autopilot operation (supported and no failures) and the pilot is not manually controlling the vehicle (standby is false), the autopilot will behave normally until a maneuver is required in the event of a hazard (sensor is not good).

Requirement Item 1:
    Requirement Description: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
    Original Text: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).

Requirement Item 2:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).

Requirement Item 3:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good.

Requirement Item 4:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is not good.
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is not good.

Requirement Item 5:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).

Requirement Item 6:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.

Requirement Item 7:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.

Requirement Item 8:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control (not standby).
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control (not standby).

Requirement Item 9:
    Requirement Description: The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to MANEUVER when a failure occurs (apfail).
    Original Text: The autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to MANEUVER when a failure occurs (apfail).

Requirement Item 10:
    Requirement Description: The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to FAULT when limits are exceeded.
    Original Text: The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to FAULT when limits are exceeded.

Requirement Item 11:
    Requirement Description: The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request).
    Original Text: The sensor shall change states from NOMINAL to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request).

Requirement Item 12:
    Requirement Description: The sensor shall change states from FAULT to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request) and limits are not exceeded (not limits).
    Original Text: The sensor shall change states from FAULT to TRANSITION when the autopilot is not requesting support (not request) and limits are not exceeded (not limits).

Requirement Item 13:
    Requirement Description: The sensor shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the autopilot is requesting support (request) and the autopilot reports the correct active mode (mode).
    Original Text: The sensor shall change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the autopilot is requesting support (request) and the autopilot reports the correct active mode (mode).

Variable 1:
    Variable Name: standby
    Type: boolean
    Description: True when the pilot is in control of the vehicle
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 2:
    Variable Name: apfail
    Type: boolean
    Description: Failure indication from an external source
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 3:
    Variable Name: supported
    Type: boolean
    Description: Indication of general health of system, must be True to enable operation
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 4:
    Variable Name: limits
    Type: boolean
    Description: External indication to sensor on faulty condition impeding safe operation.
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 5:
    Variable Name: pullup
    Type: boolean
    Description: True when the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state
    Port Type: Output
    Is Constant: No

Test Configuration 1: t = 10

**Complete Step 2:** 

Based on the extracted requirement results, generate the actions that exist in the system:
output the typical actions within the system input domain.
An action is an excitation of the system in the form of a temporal input sequence.

**Output Format**(Just output the aciton set, do not output any other characters):
Action 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description:
Input Constraint:
Temporal Constraint:

Action 2:
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description:
Input Constraint:
Temporal Constraint:
...

**Example**

Action Example 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description: Light throttle press in 3s, where “light” means throttle < 0.3
Input Constraint: throttle < 0.3
Temporal Constraint: duration <= 3

Action Example 2:
(If throttle is a parameterized variable defined by PCHIP control points, with control points c1, c2, c3)
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description: Throttle gradually increases, and no braking
Input Constraint: c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3 ∧ brake == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

**Key Notes 1: Form of Constraints**

1. The allowed symbols in constraints include `<, <=, ==, >, >=, ∧,(),abs()`.(`∨` is not not allowed, action must be atomic)
   Forms like `a < b < c` are not allowed; only forms like `a < b ∧ b < c` are permitted.
2. Input constraints must not contain natural language, and expressions such as `ia(t)` are not allowed.
   Use `ia` directly instead.
3. Input constraints should contain **only** model input variables, control-point variables from the test configuration,
   Model output variables or other constructed variables are not allowed.
4. If a model input variable is parameterized into control-point variables, the input constraint should include **only** the control-point variables, without including the original variable.
5. Temporal constraints specify constraints on the actual duration of the action, for example: `duration <= k`,
   where `duration` refers to the action duration, and `k` should be a constant or an input variable.
   If no specific constraint exists, use `true`.
6. Temporal constraints and test configuration constraints should **not** appear in the input constraint.
7. The test configuration may assign a constant value to an input variable; in this case, that variable does not need to be considered again in the input constraint.

**Key Notes 2: Principles of Action Definition**

1. The occurrence of an action **must not have preconditions**.
   For example, an action must not require the system to already be in a certain state.
   System states should be triggered by one or more actions or sequences of actions.
2. If symmetric signals exist among input variables, action merging is not allowed.
   A complete list of actions must be explicitly enumerated.
3. The input constraints of actions must not overlap.
   All actions in the action set must be pairwise mutually exclusive.

**Key Notes 3: Format Correctness**

1. If an action generated during analysis does not satisfy the required constraint form,
   that action must **not** be included in the final action set.
   In this task, **output format correctness has the highest priority**.

Action 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description: All inputs are false.
Input Constraint: standby == 0 ∧ apfail == 0 ∧ supported == 0 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 2:

Semantic Description: Only `standby` is true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 0 ∧ supported == 0 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Temporal Constraint: true

Temporal Constraint: true

Semantic Description: Only `limits` is true.
Input Constraint: standby == 0 ∧ apfail == 0 ∧ supported == 0 ∧ limits == 1
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 6:

Semantic Description: `standby` and `apfail` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 0 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 7:

Semantic Description: `standby` and `supported` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 0 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Temporal Constraint: true

Input Constraint: standby == 0 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 10:
Action Name: A10
Semantic Description: `apfail` and `limits` are true.

Temporal Constraint: true

Action 11:

Semantic Description: `supported` and `limits` are true.

Temporal Constraint: true

Action 12:
Action Name: A12
Semantic Description: `standby`, `apfail`, and `supported` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 13:
Action Name: A13
Semantic Description: `standby`, `apfail`, and `limits` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 0 ∧ limits == 1
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 14:
Action Name: A14
Semantic Description: `standby`, `supported`, and `limits` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 0 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 1
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 15:
Action Name: A15
Semantic Description: `apfail`, `supported`, and `limits` are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 0 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 1
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 16:
Action Name: A16
Semantic Description: All inputs are true.
Input Constraint: standby == 1 ∧ apfail == 1 ∧ supported == 1 ∧ limits == 1
Temporal Constraint: true

Applying the concept of combinatorial equivalence classes, 
compute the Cartesian product of the atomic condition in the input and temporal constraints to discover additional input patterns 
and generate an refined action set

Complete Step 3: Based on the extracted requirement results, generate the scenarios that exist in the system. Each scenario should be composed of actions.
Based on the extracted requirement items and the action set above, continue to complete the following tasks:
According to the specified format, and based on the given test configuration, randomly generate 20 scenarios that 
together cover all requirement items and all actions.

**Scenario Definition**
Let $\mathbb{A}$ denote a finite action set.
A \emph{scenario} is defined as a tuple
\[
\mathcal{S} = (\mathbf{A}, \sigma_S, \rho),
\]
where:
\begin{itemize}
  \item $\mathbf{A} = \langle \mathcal{A}_1, \mathcal{A}_2, \dots, \mathcal{A}_K \rangle$
  is an ordered sequence of actions, with $\mathcal{A}_k \in \mathbb{A}$;
  \item $\sigma_S$ denotes the semantic description of the scenario;
  \item $\rho$ denotes the identifier of the associated requirement(s).
\end{itemize}


### Output Format((Do not output any other characters)):
#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: (natural language description)
* Executable Action Set: (action names, separated by commas, e.g., A1,A2)
* Scenario Action Sequence: (ordered list enclosed in `[ ]`, where each element is an action name, e.g., [A1,A2])
* Associated Requirement:

#S2:
...

### Scenario Example:
#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: First lightly press the throttle (assumed to correspond to action A1), then lightly press the brake (assumed to correspond to action A2), and then press the throttle aggressively (assumed to correspond to action A3)
* Executable Actions: A1, A2, A3
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A1, A2, A3]
* Associated Requirement: (fill in the relevant requirement IDs)


### Key Notes 1:
The scenario semantic description should express a **feasible and random combination of action sequences**.
The scenario action sequence should be **consistent with the scenario semantic description**.

#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Apply input combination with all inputs false.
* Executable Action Set: A1
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A1]
* Associated Requirement: R4, R8, R12

#S2:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Apply input combination with only standby true.
* Executable Action Set: A2
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A2]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R5, R6

#S3:

* Executable Action Set: A3
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A3]
* Associated Requirement: R9

#S4:

* Executable Action Set: A4
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A4]
* Associated Requirement: R3, R7

#S5:

* Executable Action Set: A5
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A5]
* Associated Requirement: R10

#S6:

* Executable Action Set: A6

* Associated Requirement: R2, R5, R6, R11

#S7:

* Executable Action Set: A7
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A7]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R5, R6, R13

#S8:

* Scenario Action Sequence: [A8]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R5, R6

* Executable Action Set: A9

* Associated Requirement: R3, R7

* Scenario Semantic Description: Apply input combination with apfail and limits true.
* Executable Action Set: A10
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A10]

* Associated Requirement: R2, R4, R5, R6, R8, R12

#S18:

* Executable Action Set: A10, A11

* Associated Requirement: R1, R10
